title: Phlegmariurus saururus
taxonomy: Phlegmariurus saururus is classified as a species of plant within the division Lycopodiophyta, which includes all the lycopod or club moss plants. It belongs to the family Huperziaceae, a group of plants that are typically small and non-vascular. Phlegmariurus saururus is further categorized as a genus within the family. The exact classification is as follows: Phlegmariurus saururus (Herter) B. Øllg. is a species of plant within the family Huperziaceae. It is native to Mexico.

intro: Phlegmariurus saururus, a species of club moss, is a type of lycopod that belongs to the family Lycopodiaceae. Traditionally, Phlegmariurus saururus has been used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. In herbal medicine, Phlegmariurus saururus is commonly prepared as a decoction or tincture, often combined with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects, particularly in traditional Chinese and Ayurvedic medicine.
